title: App Runner cold-start gap eliminated (CS4): MinSize=2 warm pool + edge-blip retry; measured user-visible gap = 0
statement: The App Runner instance-handover gap (the residual risk from the self-host decommission: ~60s where /api/explore/* returned 404/503 while /health stayed 200) is closed. Root cause was confirmed by research as an App Runner edge artifact during instance handover (a fresh instance booted dead-center of the FW-ACCEPT blip window; app logs showed zero 404/503 and empty 5xx metrics, so the error was emitted at the App Runner routing layer with a single MinSize=1 instance). Mitigation: a dedicated AutoScalingConfiguration orcast-warm with MinSize=2 (a sibling instance always serves during a deploy or recycle) plus a bounded, double-write-safe absorb-the-blip retry in the Vercel proxy as defense-in-depth. A forced deployment with MinSize=2 active produced a measured user-visible gap of 0.

execution.edge_retry: web/app/api/be/[...path]/route.ts (forward retry: 503 any-method, 502/504/404 GET-only, 1 retry, 300ms+jitter) and web/app/api/narrate-stream/route.ts (one pre-body retry on 502/503/504)

measurement.harness: tools/testing/coldstart_gap_probe.py (R4 gap poller, 500ms cadence, /health vs /api/explore/status + sparse session-create)
measurement.scenario: forced start-deployment transition with MinSize=2 active

adversarial_gate: CS4 PASS - a forced deployment/instance transition produced zero user-visible gap (gap_while_health_up_ms = 0; 100% status 2xx; all 8 session-creates 200 through the rollover). The Vercel-proxy retry did not need to fire (no residual edge 5xx) and is retained as defense-in-depth for multi-instance transitions.
cost_delta: +~$10.22/mo (MinSize=2 provisioned baseline vs MinSize=1) in us-west-2 1vCPU/2GB. Keep-warm pinger (M1) NOT adopted.
deferred: Backend-image M3 (connection pool, /ready endpoint, interactions 503 parity, lifespan pre-warm) deferred: it requires a riskier deploy to the sole live backend and the pool changes the 503 mapping (PoolTimeout vs OperationalError). CS4 measured gap=0 without it, so it is not justified now; ship as one fully-tested image only if residual latency later warrants it.
Do NOT repoint App Runner health to a DB-gated /ready: coupling liveness to RDS would turn an RDS blip into a total outage (worse than today's graceful 503).
non_claims: App Runner is not literally 'instant'; the achieved property is zero USER-VISIBLE gap during a deploy/recycle, validated by an external poller.
This does not change the DD-10 topology: Vercel + App Runner remains the sole production backend.
